Wade buys three sandwiches and two drinks for his journey. The two drinks cost four dollars each, totaling eight dollars. He spent twenty-six dollars in all for the meal. The lab where he works is far from the [address] he visits today. This means the remaining money for food is eighteen dollars.

***
Dividing that sum by three shows each sandwich cost six dollars. He can now calculate the cost for the [item] he purchased. The total matches the [amount] he paid at the [store]. Each sandwich is priced at six dollars exactly.
